_meta.description: AstroPay image library — extracted from Figma Images page. Source of truth for ALL imagery used by paid media, email, and in-app creatives.
rules: ABSOLUTE RULE — every image used by any agent MUST come from one of the six sections on this page: Traveler, Places, Freelancer, Digital nomad, Product Mockup, Product. No external URLs. No free-floating canvas frames. No images from any other Figma file or page.
Never use findAll() on this page (200+ nodes = timeout) — always findOne() by exact name.
Image must match the campaign category — see selection_rules below.
Cache the imageHash immediately after retrieval — never re-fetch in the same task.

Product and Product Mockup are exchange-campaign sources only. Use Product (flat 1:1 product images) for in-feed creatives where the screen is shown clean; use Product Mockup (full mockup previews) for hero / fullbg layouts where a phone-like artifact is desired.
_meta.sync_note: Manual update on 2026-04-30 — page restructured: legacy 'exchange_campaign' section removed; replaced by two new sections: 'Product' (flat exchange images, _PTBR / _ES variants) and 'Product Mockup' (mockup previews). Section IDs renumbered to 939:xxxxx range. The free-floating editable mockup wrapper frames + screens that exist outside sections are NOT considered sources of truth and must not be used by agents.

traveler.description: Lifestyle travel imagery — airports, beaches, global destinations, aerial shorelines. Aspirational, on-the-go.

places.description: Destinations, cities, venues, lifestyle locations. Premium, destination-driven. Coffee shops, hotel exteriors, urban scenes.

selection_examples.name: coffe 03 - El cafe de la esquina 1

freelancer.description: Freelancers, entrepreneurs, SMB owners working independently. Laptop, home office, never generic fintech stock.

digital_nomad.description: Remote workers, laptops in cafes, professionals on the go. Warm, relatable, urban — bridges traveler and freelancer.

product.description: Flat 1:1 product images of the AstroPay exchange flow per language pair. Use for in-feed creatives where the screen is the focal point and no phone mockup is desired. Variants: _PTBR (Brazilian Portuguese UI) and _ES (Spanish UI).
product._use: Use these instead of Product Mockup whenever the design calls for a clean, flat exchange screen as image content (e.g., 1:1 design_2, 4:5 design_3, in-app illustration headers).

product_mockup.description: Mockup-style previews of the AstroPay exchange flow — phone-frame artifact baked in. Use as image content for fullbg layouts (design_4 / design_5) where the mockup serves as the hero visual. Same six currency pairs as Product, in mockup form.
product_mockup._use: Use for hero/fullbg layouts (design_4, design_5) and for Product Mockup retargeting creatives where the phone-shaped silhouette adds context. For clean in-feed flat images, use the Product section instead.

selection_rules.BR campaigns: digital_nomad or freelancer — warm, relatable, urban
selection_rules.Exchange (retargeting): product (flat) for in-feed creatives where the UI is the focal point, OR digital_nomad / freelancer lifestyle imagery for context-led layouts. NEVER travel-themed (Paris cafe, airport, suitcase) — that suggests a trip campaign, not currency conversion.
selection_rules.Exchange (awareness / hero): product_mockup — match the correct currency pair (brl_to_usd, ars_to_eur, etc.) and language variant (_PTBR for BR, _ES for AR)
selection_rules.Freelance SMB: freelancer — specific, not generic fintech stock
selection_rules.PIX (AR → BR): traveler imagery is correct — campaign IS about traveling to Brazil

currency_pair_routing._note: When the brief specifies a currency pair, use this map to pick the correct image from product or product_mockup.
